panels.description: Wide angle shot: The red planet Mars glows ominously in the deep black of space, surrounded by cold stars. In the foreground, the silhouette of a massive brass Victorian telescope points toward the heavens, its lens reflecting the distant red light.

bubbles.text: I do not think enough attention is given to this matter. As Mars returns to conjunction, I anticipate a renewal of their adventure. We must be prepared.

panels.description: Medium shot: A cluttered mahogany desk filled with detailed technical blueprints. The diagrams show the trajectory of Martian cylinders and designs for heavy artillery cannons. A single quill pen rests on the parchment, ink still wet.

bubbles.text: We should define the position of their guns and watch. Perhaps we could destroy the cylinders with artillery before they even emerge.

panels.description: Wide angle: A celestial map of the solar system showing Earth, Venus, and Mars in a straight alignment with a bright sun. Glowing, sinuous markings are illustrated on the unillumined side of Venus, suggesting a second invasion.

bubbles.text: There are reasons to believe the Martians have landed on Venus. Sinuous markings appeared on both disks during their alignment with the sun.

panels.description: Medium shot: A diverse group of Victorian citizens—a laborer, a lady, and a soldier—standing together in a cobblestone town square. They look up at the night sky with unified, somber expressions of awe and lingering fear. Dramatic overhead lighting.

bubbles.text: Our views of the human future are forever modified. We can no longer regard this planet as a secure abiding place; we are not alone in the universe.

panels.description: Medium shot: A group of Victorian scientists huddled around a captured piece of Martian technology, their faces illuminated by a strange, inner glow. They look determined, representing the new 'commonweal of mankind'.

bubbles.text: The invasion has robbed us of our decadent confidence. It has brought enormous gifts to science and promoted the commonweal of mankind.

panels.description: Wide shot: A conceptual vision of a golden 'thread of life'—a stream of light—extending from the small, blue Earth and reaching out toward the thick, mysterious clouds of the planet Venus in the vastness of space.

bubbles.text: If they can reach Venus, so can we. Life may spread from this little seed bed throughout the inanimate vastness of sidereal space.

panels.description: Wide angle: The Narrator, a weary man with greyed hair, sits at a desk in his dark study. He is lit by a single green-shaded lamp. Behind him, a window shows a peaceful, moonlit valley, but the room itself feels hollow and lonely. Heavy shadows.

bubbles.text: I sit in my study by lamplight, and suddenly I see again the healing valley below set with writhing flames... and feel the house behind me empty and desolate.
